0: Hands-On Code: Memory Management in Google
2: The Google Agent Developer Kit (ADK) offers a structured method for managing
3: context and memory, including components for practical application. A solid grasp of
4: ADK's Session, State, and Memory is vital for building agents that need to retain
6: Just as in human interactions, agents require the ability to recall previous exchanges
7: to conduct coherent and natural conversations. ADK simplifies context management
8: through three core concepts and their associated services.
9: Every interaction with an agent can be considered a unique conversation thread.
10: Agents might need to access data from earlier interactions. ADK structures this as
13: Session: An individual chat thread that logs messages and actions (Events) for
14: that specific interaction, also storing temporary data (State) relevant to that
17: State (session.state): Data stored within a Session, containing information
18: relevant only to the current, active chat thread.
20: Memory: A searchable repository of information sourced from various past
21: chats or external sources, serving as a resource for data retrieval beyond the
23: ADK provides dedicated services for managing critical components essential for
24: building complex, stateful, and context-aware agents. The SessionService manages
25: chat threads (Session objects) by handling their initiation, recording, and termination,
26: while the MemoryService oversees the storage and retrieval of long-term knowledge
28: Both the SessionService and MemoryService offer various configuration options,
29: allowing users to choose storage methods based on application needs. In-memory
30: options are available for testing purposes, though data will not persist across restarts.
31: For persistent storage and scalability, ADK also supports database and cloud-based
